content.full_text:
July 14, 1978

Mr. Joseph F. Cornett
10915 Penney Avenue
Inglewood, California 90303

Dear Mr. Cornett:

Thank you for your letter dated March 28, 1978, requesting advice
on the campaign reporting obligations of your client, the Inglewood
Democratic Club.

I have enclosed a copy of an opinion issued by us in response to
a request from Francis Wilmarth, an attorney representing the
California Democratic Council. The Wilmarth Opinion is applicable
to the questions raised in your letter.

I would ask you to note particularly that receipts of the Club
are contributions only if they are "earmarked" for the making of
expenditures or contributions. Receipts are "earmarked" if the
donor knows or has reason to know that the dues he pays will be
used in whole or in part to make donations to candidates and ballot
measure campaigns or to pay for campaign activities supporting or
opposing candidates or measures. 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18215(a)
provides that "factors relevant to a determination that the donor
has reason to know that all or part of the payment will be used to
make expenditures and contributions include but are not limited to
the established practice of the person or organization with respect
to expenditures and contributions and any representations made when
the payment is solicited."

If the donors of membership dues to the Inglewood Democratic Club
have reason to know that dues payments will be used to make expendi-
tures and contributions and if the amount of such dues received and
earmarked for making contributions and expenditures equals or ex-
ceeds $500 in a calendar year the Club will become a recipient com-
mittee as defined in Government Code Section 82013(a) and will be
required to report the amount of dues payments "earmarked" for the
making of expenditures and contributions as contributions received
by the Club.

Mr. Joseph F. Cornett                    -2-                    July 14, 1978


Determining which form the Inglewood Democratic Club should use
to file as a recipient committee depends upon whether the Club
has received $50 from any one donor. If no one donor has contri-
buted $50 to the Club, the Club may use Form 450, which does not
require itemization of donors. If the Club has received $50 or
more from any donor, the Club must use Form 420 and report the
names and other identifying information about those persons who
have given it $50 or more.

To determine the amount of the contribution made by any donor to
the Club if the donor knows or has reason to know that any part
of his/her dues payment will be used to make contributions or
expenditures, the donor's dues payment must be apportioned on a
reasonable basis. (2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18215(d))

If you need additional information or assistance, you may call
me at 916/322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        John Giansello
                                        Political Reform Analyst

JG:bw

Enclosure

F P P C JOSEPH F. CORNETT
Attorney at Law
10915 PENNEY AVENUE
JUN 30 11 00 AM '78 INGLEWOOD, CALIF. 90303

(213) 486-6729 779-1261

June 28, 1978

Fair Political Practices Commission
107 S. Broadway, Room 7007
Los Angeles, CA   90012

RE:   Inglewood Democratic Club
      Request for an opinion

Gentlemen:

The undersigned is the chairman of the Finance Committee
of the captioned club and is a charter member and founder of
the captioned club.

Acting within the authority of the committee chair and
reviewing the Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Pro-
visions of the Political Reform Act (1978) prepared by your
commission, the undersigned determined that the captioned
club qualified as an Expenditure Committee based on the fact
that it made no contributions at the behest of any candidate
or committee.

The expenditures made by the club from January 1, 1978
up to June, 1978 have been independently made in connection
with the club's opposition to the Inglewood Gambling Ini-
tiative at a March Special Election and to certain candidates
as a consequence of the club voting to endorse those can-
didates. Since a candidate was not endorsed by the club's
membership, that candidate did not receive a contribution.

The source of income to the club has been from member-
ship fees and an officer installation dinner.  The club has
received no contributions from any other source.

The treasurer of the club has received conflicting
advice from the FPPC in that the treasurer has been advised
that the membership fees of $50.00 paid by the charter members
of the club must be reported as contributions, requiring the
club to qualify as a committee and the treasurer filing form
420, i.e., Recipient Committee Campaign Statement.

State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444

February 23, 1978


Mr. Clark L. Smithson
City Manager, La Canada Flintridge
Post Office Box 456
La Canada Flintridge, California  91011

Dear Mr. Smithson:

     Thank you for your letter of February 7, 1978, commenting
on our Regulation 18116. We appreciate the concerns with
regard to local holidays which you have expressed in your letter.
However, before we amend the regulation we would like to know
whether the current wording poses any real problems; that is,
are there any days which are local holidays but not state holi-
days which are likely to be filing dates? Offhand, it seems
unlikely that the Fridays after Thanksgiving will ever be filing
dates.

     I look forward to your comments.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]

                                        Ted Prim, Chief
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division

TP:glb

Fair Political Practices Commission
June 28, 1978
Page 2

The $50.00 paid by each charter member represents
four years of membership at $12.00 per year paid in advance.
The $2.00 is the fee paid to the California Democratic Council to affiliate the club with the CDC.

It is the opinion of the undersigned that membership
fees are in the nature of consideration paid to an organization in return for receiving the benefits and privileges
of being a member of that particular organization and are,
therefore, not political contributions. The relationship
between the club and its members is contractual regardless
of the fact that the club is politically oriented. If
membership fees are construed as contributions, then any
contributions made to any candidate or any political organization or committee would give rise to contractual relationships with the attendant consequences.

To clarify this question and to give guidance to the
club and its treasurer, the undersigned in his capacity as
chairman of the Finance Committee hereby requests an opinion
from the FPPC on the following questions:

1. Are membership fees considered political contributions by members of the Inglewood Democratic Club?

2. Is the Inglewood Democratic Club, based on the
facts stated above, a Recipient Committee or
an Expenditure Committee or both?

3. Assuming that the Inglewood Democratic Club is
a committee, what forms should the treasurer file
with the FPPC to report the club's expenditures
as described above?

The undersigned would appreciate your earliest response
to this request for an opinion.

Very truly yours,

Joseph F. Cornett

JFC:vrs
cc: FPPC
Ms. Martina Blount-Treasurer
Mr. Roosevelt F. Dorn-President

sections.question: Are membership fees considered political contributions by members of the Inglewood Democratic Club? Is the Inglewood Democratic Club, based on the facts stated above, a Recipient Committee or an Expenditure Committee or both? Assuming that the Inglewood Democratic Club is a committee, what forms should the treasurer file with the FPPC to report the club's expenditures as described above?
sections.conclusion: If the donors of membership dues to the Inglewood Democratic Club have reason to know that dues payments will be used to make expenditures and contributions and if the amount of such dues received and earmarked for making contributions and expenditures equals or exceeds $500 in a calendar year the Club will become a recipient committee. Determining which form to use depends on whether the Club has received $50 from any donor; if no donor contributed $50 or more, Form 450 may be used; if any donor contributed $50 or more, Form 420 must be used with itemization of those donors.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether membership dues paid to the Inglewood Democratic Club constitute political contributions and what reporting obligations the club must meet as a recipient or expenditure committee.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Membership dues are contributions only if earmarked for political expenditures or contributions, which is determined by whether donors know or should know the dues will be used for campaign activities. If earmarked dues total $500+ annually, the club becomes a recipient committee and must file either Form 450 or Form 420 depending on the donation threshold.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request (June 28, 1978) and FPPC response letter (July 14, 1978). Clear identification of question sections in incoming request letter and conclusion in response letter. Analytical framework provided for determining what constitutes earmarked contributions under Cal. Adm. Code Section 18215.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Are membership fees considered political contributions by members of the Inglewood Democratic Club? Is the Inglewood Democratic Club, based on the facts stated above, a Recipient Committee or an Expenditure Committee or both? Assuming that the Inglewood Democratic Club is a committee, what forms should the treasurer file with the FPPC to report the club's expenditures as described above?

CONCLUSION: If the donors of membership dues to the Inglewood Democratic Club have reason to know that dues payments will be used to make expenditures and contributions and if the amount of such dues received and earmarked for making contributions and expenditures equals or exceeds $500 in a calendar year the Club will become a recipient committee. Determining which form to use depends on whether the Club has received $50 from any donor; if no donor contributed $50 or more, Form 450 may be used; if any donor contributed $50 or more, Form 420 must be used with itemization of those donors.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to Inglewood Democratic Club inquiry regarding whether membership dues are political contributions and what reporting requirements apply, referencing an applicable prior opinion and clarifying that dues are contributions only if earmarked for political expenditures or contributions.
